feed.title: Zoom Developers Blog
feed.subtitle: Blog RSS feed for developers.zoom.us
feed.rights: All rights reserved 2026, Zoom Video Communications, Inc.

entry.title: 4 methods to protect sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) data in Zoom Virtual Agent integrations
entry.summary: Tags: ZVA, Security, Marketplace, API | Learn four proven methods to filter and hide Personally Identifiable Information (PII) when integrating Zoom Virtual Agent with external APIs.

entry.title: Build real-time audio and video deepfake detection with Zoom RTMS
entry.summary: Tags: RTMS, AI, Zoom Apps | Stream individual participant audio and video from a Zoom Meeting into AI detection services and show the results inside a Zoom App.

entry.title: Build multilingual experiences with the Zoom Translator API
entry.summary: Tags: announcement, api, ai-services | Translate support messages, UI strings, event Q&A, templates, and feedback using the Zoom Translator API with fast, inline translation and asynchronous batch processing.

entry.title: Extract Intelligence from Transcripts with the Zoom Summarizer API
entry.summary: Tags: announcement, api, ai-services | Turn thousands of meeting and call transcripts into structured summaries, action items, and recaps using the Zoom Summarizer API—batch processing up to 10,000 files per job from S3, with webhooks and structured JSON output for automation.

entry.title: Zoom AI Services: Introducing Summarizer and Translator APIs
entry.summary: Tags: announcement, api, ai-services | Zoom AI Services now includes two new APIs—Summarizer for structured meeting and call summaries, and Translator for text translation across 9 languages—joining Scribe to complete the speech, language, and translation suite.

entry.title: Apply Real-Time Video Filters in Android with Zoom Video SDK
entry.summary: Tags: Android, Raw Data | Use the Android Video SDK pre-processor to run a GPU shader on every frame and ship a custom video effect to every participant in the session.

entry.title: Creating a subsession (breakout room) in Zoom Video SDK for iOS
entry.summary: Tags: iOS, subsession, breakout-room | A guide to splits the main session into smaller independent session known as subsession for participants to self-join, and can later return to the main session.

entry.title: Use least privilege to improve Zoom app installation and adoption
entry.summary: Tags: Guide | Learn how to apply the principle of least privilege when building Zoom apps

entry.title: Building advanced applications using Zoom Quality of Service Subscription (QSS)
entry.summary: Tags: qss, qos | Learn how to leverage Zoom Video SDK's QSS features to optimize video and audio performance in advanced application scenarios.

entry.title: Introducing PKCE support for public and confidential clients
entry.summary: Tags: Guide, Annoucement, Public PKCE | Learn how to implement OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow with PKCE for native and browser-based applications

entry.title: Integrating Zoom Meeting SDK into Blazor
entry.summary: Tags: Meeting SDK | How to integrate Meeting SDK into Blazor, a blog by a Zoom community member

entry.title: Building powerful Zoom Phone experiences with the Zoom Apps SDK
entry.summary: Tags: zoom apps, phone | Learn how to use the Zoom Apps SDK for Phone with OAuth, REST APIs, and Webhooks to create seamless in-call experiences.

entry.title: Introducing Realtime Media Streams for Zoom Contact Center
entry.summary: Tags: RTMS, contact center, Announcement | Learn about RTMS and how it works with Zoom Contact Center

entry.title: Collaborate together with Video SDK whiteboards
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial, Video SDK, web | Build a shared whiteboard workflow in Video SDK Web with presenter controls, viewer sync, and PDF export.

entry.title: Add phone call support to Video SDK apps
entry.summary: Tags: web, pstn, video-sdk | Learn how to integrate PSTN features into your applications using Zoom Video SDK, including dial out and dial in capabilities.

entry.title: Real-time video effects in iOS with Zoom Video SDK
entry.summary: Tags: iOS, raw data | A guide to quickly pre-process raw video data in your iOS app

entry.title: Announcing Agent Skills for the Zoom Developer Platform
entry.summary: Tags: announcement, ai | Agent Skills are now available on GitHub, giving coding agents the context they need to build faster and more accurately with Zoom.

entry.title: Introducing Zoom AI Services
entry.summary: Tags: announcement, api | Enterprise-grade intelligence APIs for speech, vision, and language built for scale

entry.title: Voice to insights: modernize customer Support with the Zoom Scribe API
entry.summary: Tags: announcement, api, transcription | Transcribe customer support calls at scale with the Zoom Scribe API—batch and real-time transcription, speaker diarization, and webhooks so you can power sentiment analysis, keyword detection, and QA workflows.

entry.title: Improved React support for Video SDK
entry.summary: Tags: announcement, web, react | Announcing our new React library that makes integrating Zoom Video SDK into React applications easier than ever

entry.title: Next.js video conferencing app with the Zoom Video SDK
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial, getting started, web | This guide showcases adding video conferencing to your Next.js/React website

entry.title: Implementing Gemini Live API to use alongside the Zoom Video SDK
entry.summary: Tags: web, video-sdk | Build a simple React App that uses the new Gemini Live API and Zoom Video SDK.

entry.title: Choosing the right attribution path for your Zoom app
entry.summary: Tags: Authorization, RTMS, Meeting SDK, Security | Learn how to choose the right authorization model for your Zoom app, including RTMS, OBF, and ZAK, based on attribution, visibility, and media access needs.

entry.title: Implementing sentiment analysis with Zoom Video SDK
entry.summary: Tags: web, live transcription, rtms, video-sdk | Add Sentiment Analysis to your apps using Zoom Video SDK features for both client-side and server-side use cases

entry.title: Attendee makes capturing RTMS meeting data simple
entry.summary: Tags: Open Source, AI | Discover how Attendee pairs with Zoom’s Real Time Media Streams (RTMS) to capture, process, and display live meeting data, making it simple to build secure, self‑hosted, real‑time meeting assistants.

entry.title: Launching the RTMS SDK for Node.js and Python
entry.summary: Tags: guide, getting started | v1.0 of the Node.js and Python SDKs for Zoom RTMS. Access live meeting audio, video, and transcripts with a callback-driven API.

entry.title: Video SDK adds support for Realtime Media Streams (RTMS)
entry.summary: Tags: Announcement, AI | Zoom announces Realtime Media Streams (RTMS) support for Video SDK. Developers can now access per-participant audio, video, transcripts via WebSocket without automated clients.

entry.title: Transcribe Video SDK sessions with Realtime Media Streams
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial, rtms | Use Zoom Realtime Media Streams (RTMS) to capture audio from Video SDK sessions and transcribe it on your server.

entry.title: Video SDK Web UI Toolkit is open source
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial, web, uitoolkit | Guide for building with the open-sourced Web UI Toolkit

entry.title: Building an AI-powered meeting evaluator with RTMS and InWorld Runtime
entry.summary: Tags: RTMS, AI, Zoom Apps | See how Inworld AI combined Zoom Realtime Media Streams (RTMS) with their AI Runtime to build a realtime agent performance evaluator that analyzes both audio and video streams.

entry.title: Call history data model: segments and nodes
entry.summary: Tags: guide, getting started, phone | Use Zoom Phone API to build a cloud telephony solution for your business.

entry.title: Get meeting transcripts using native WebSockets with RTMS
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial, RTMS, WebSockets, getting started | Build a small WebSocket server that receives live transcripts from Zoom Realtime Media Streams (RTMS) without using the SDK.

entry.title: Add closed captions to user videos in real-time with Zoom Video SDK
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial, video sdk, web | Learn how to turn live transcription into burned-in captions on video and screen share using media processors in the Zoom Video SDK for Web.

entry.title: Display multiple screen shares simultaneously in Video SDK
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial, Video SDK, web | Learn how to render multiple screen shares simultaneously using the new attachShareView and detachShareView APIs in Video SDK Web v2.2.10

entry.title: Transitioning to On Behalf Of (OBF) tokens in Meeting SDK apps
entry.summary: Tags: meeting SDK | Zoom is introducing a new authorization requirement for Meeting SDK apps joining meetings outside their account. This update enhances user trust by requiring apps to join on behalf of a user using an OAuth-based OBF token.

entry.title: Automating employee celebration messages using Workvivo API, Zoom webhook and AWS
entry.summary: Tags: Guide | Learn how to build a serverless automation system using AWS Lambda to celebrate employee work anniversaries across Workvivo and Zoom platforms.

entry.summary: Tags: getting started, contact center | In this tutorial article, Donte Small provides a step-by-step guide on getting started with the Zoom Contact Center App using the Zoom App SDK.

entry.summary: Tags: Announcement, UX, AI | Explore how Realtime Media Streams (RTMS) enables seamless, trusted AI meeting apps with direct, real-time access to Zoom data.

entry.title: BrightHire has grown usage 3x since launching the BrightHire Interview Assistant app with Zoom Meetings
entry.summary: Tags: App of the month | BrightHire built an intelligent interview assistant app with Zoom to transform hiring experiences. By combining AI and Zoom's platform, they enable every interviewer with capabilities like pre-populated questions, AI-generated notes, and integrations with applicant tracking systems. Usage grew 3x since launch.

entry.summary: Tags: guide, web | The goal of this article is to guide you in implementing cross origin isolation and enable shared array buffer.

entry.title: How to limit who can install my Marketplace App
entry.summary: Tags: Guide | Control who installs your published Zoom app using landing page restrictions. Step-by-step guide to limit marketplace app access to specific users.

entry.title: How to embed Zoom into a website
entry.summary: Tags: Guide | Compare Zoom's two web SDKs, Meeting SDK for embedding Zoom meetings vs Video SDK for custom video experiences. Complete developer guide with use cases.

entry.title: Migrating from collecting developer credentials to a supported flow
entry.summary: Tags: Guide | Zoom policy on developer credentials and alternative integration solutions. Explore OAuth, API users, Meeting SDK, and Video SDK for compliant development.

entry.title: JWT deprecation guide
entry.summary: Tags: guide, JWT | The JWT deprecation guide informs users about the deprecation process of JWT app types in the Zoom Marketplace. On June 1st, 2023, new JWT apps creation was disabled. By September 8, 2023, all JWT apps will reach their end-of-life, requiring migration to server-to-server OAuth or OAuth app types. Users must ensure third-party developers migrate their apps or work on updates to avoid disruptions. The guide provides steps to identify and migrate JWT apps, along with disabling or removing them when no longer needed. Users can seek support from the Developer Forum or Zoom Developer Open Hours.

entry.title: Level Up Your Productivity with Reminder Apps for Team Chat
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial | In this blog, we'll build a Team Chat App that allows you to send reminders to yourself throughout your day using the popular and open-source backend Appwrite. You'll learn how to take your Apps for Team Chat to the next level using functions alongside the composability of Team Chat messages. Let’s get started!

entry.title: Creating a virtual classroom with the Zoom Video SDK
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial, web | Follow along with Developer Advocate Rehema Armorer as she describes her process of creating an online learning environment using the Video SDK.

entry.title: Exploring the 2023 Zoom Developer Summit
entry.summary: Tags: event | Dive into the highlights of the action-packed 2023 Zoom Developer Summit! This article recaps the event's 8 technical sessions, featuring insights from Zoom's Developer Relations team, covering various aspects of the Zoom Developer Platform. Attendees explored using the Meeting SDK with AWS virtual participant framework, building apps for Team Chat, leveraging QSS and APIs for a comprehensive Meetings dashboard, and more. The summit showcased the possibilities offered by the Developer Platform and celebrated the ingenuity of the developer community. Get inspired to build with Zoom's Developer Platform by accessing on-demand recordings of the sessions and apply to become an ISV Partner for dedicated developer support. Stay connected with the Zoom developer community through the forum and explore the latest demos on YouTube. Start building innovative solutions with Zoom's video and meeting capabilities today!

entry.title: How to fix hanging behavior of the Meeting SDK Electron sample app
entry.summary: Tags: Troubleshooting, Electron | Troubleshoot Meeting SDK demo app freezing after SDKAuth button. Fix JWT validation, Node/Electron versions, dependencies, and common setup issues.

entry.title: Troubleshooting Meeting SDK signature validation
entry.summary: Tags: troubleshooting | placeholder description

entry.title: Best practices using the Meeting SDK in an iFrame
entry.summary: Tags: Best practices, web | Embed Zoom Meeting SDK for web in iFrame with best practices guide. Covers HTTPS requirements, permissions, SharedArrayBuffer, and responsive design tips.

entry.title: The Zoom GraphQL API playground - Your new favorite development tool
entry.summary: Tags: announcement | The Zoom GraphQL API Playground is a powerful in-browser IDE that enhances development with GraphQL queries and mutations. It offers a rich user interface, auto-complete, and real-time query validation. Developers can access embedded documentation, test queries, and merge fragments. The tool streamlines development, improves code quality, and ensures API functionality. Joining the Zoom GraphQL API beta provides early access to new features and the chance to shape the tool's future. Experience the impact of the GraphQL playground and elevate your development game.

entry.title: Best practices using the Meeting SDK in an Android WebView
entry.summary: Tags: Best practices, android, web | Embed Zoom Meeting SDK for web in Android WebView for smaller package size. Complete guide with permissions, configuration, and implementation code.

entry.title: Best practices using the Meeting SDK in an iOS WebView
entry.summary: Tags: Best practices, iOS, web | Embed Zoom Meeting SDK for web in iOS WebView for smaller package size. Complete guide with Info.plist configuration, permissions, and Swift code examples.

entry.title: Best practices using the Video SDK in an Android WebView
entry.summary: Tags: Best practices, Android, web | Embed Zoom Video SDK for web in Android WebView for smaller package size. Complete guide with permissions, configuration, and implementation code.

entry.title: Best practices using the Video SDK in an iFrame
entry.summary: Tags: Best practices, Web | Embed Zoom Video SDK for web in iFrame with best practices guide. Covers HTTPS requirements, permissions, SharedArrayBuffer, and responsive design tips.

entry.title: Best practices using the Video SDK in an iOS WebView
entry.summary: Tags: Best practices, iOS, web | Embed Zoom Video SDK for web in iOS WebView for smaller package size. Complete guide with Info.plist configuration, permissions, and Swift code examples.

entry.title: How to customize the Meeting SDK for web
entry.summary: Tags: Guide | Customize Zoom Meeting SDK for web interface. Learn to hide buttons like invite function with code examples and understand customization limitations.

entry.title: Using ngrok to receive Zoom Events
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial | This tutorial by Elisa Levet guides users on setting up Zoom webhooks using ngrok as a secure URL to receive events from a Zoom account. The guide provides steps to implement Zoom webhooks, create a sample app, validate the endpoint URL, and receive events securely. The tutorial uses the Webhook-sample-node.js app available on GitHub and covers the necessary configurations in the Zoom Marketplace. It also explains how to trigger webhook validation, handle challenge-response checks, and ensure secure event reception in the sample app.

entry.title: Device authorization
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial | Learn about Zoom's new Device Authorization feature designed to enhance end-user experience when granting app access to Zoom user data across multiple devices. This article presents a simple one-step process using the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant, allowing users to authorize app access on different devices without the need to re-enter user codes or log in again.

entry.title: Access token change FAQ
entry.summary: Tags: Announcement | test

entry.title: Build with Me! - Getting Started with the Zoom Video SDK
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial, getting started, web | In this tutorial article, Rehema Armorer provides a step-by-step guide on getting started with the Zoom Video SDK. The article covers building a simplified version of the Web Video SDK app using React and demonstrates how to access video, audio, and screen-sharing functionalities. The tutorial highlights the tech stack used, including React for the frontend, Node.js with Express for the backend, and the Zoom Video SDK for creating, managing, and joining sessions.

entry.title: FAQ on Privacy/Embedded TDD
entry.summary: Tags: App Submission | New Zoom App Marketplace data privacy requirements explained. FAQ guide for developers updating TDD attestations and handling user data compliance.

entry.title: What I learned implementing PKCE for serverless Zoom Apps
entry.summary: Tags: guide | The article discusses Max's experience implementing PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) for serverless Zoom Apps. PKCE is essential for secure OAuth authorization and preventing CSRF attacks.

entry.title: How to approach creating a Marketplace App Test Plan
entry.summary: Tags: App submission | test

entry.title: What is a Test Plan and how should you approach creating it
entry.summary: Tags: App Submission, FAQ | Create effective test plans for Zoom Marketplace app submissions. Essential guide for developers with requirements, common mistakes, and review tips.

entry.title: Setting up a Postgres database to receive Zoom webhooks
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial | The article guides readers through setting up a PostgreSQL database to receive Zoom webhooks. It begins by creating a webhook-only app in the Zoom App Marketplace and configuring event subscriptions with ngrok to expose the local server to the internet. The tutorial requires Node.js, ngrok, PostgreSQL, a Zoom account, and webhook-only app credentials. Readers then clone a sample app, install dependencies, and set up the database. The tutorial covers creating a role and database in PostgreSQL, creating a table, and connecting the app to the database. It concludes with instructions to run the app, enabling users to see events printed in the console and stored in the PostgreSQL database.

entry.title: Troubleshooting joining a meeting with the Meeting SDK for Windows
entry.summary: Tags: Troubleshooting', Windows | Troubleshoot Zoom Meeting SDK join issues with this comprehensive guide. Debug event callbacks, authentication errors, and connection problems step-by-step

entry.title: How the Zoom Meeting SDKs are versioned
entry.summary: Tags: Versioning | Understand Zoom Meeting SDK versioning scheme, backwards compatibility, new features, breaking changes, and minimum support versions for smooth upgrades.

entry.title: How to reduce APK size after adding the Meeting SDK to your project
entry.summary: Tags: Android, Best practices | Reduce Android APK size when using Zoom SDK. Learn ABI filtering and native library optimization techniques to minimize app size with code examples.

entry.title: How the Zoom Video SDKs are versioned
entry.summary: Tags: Versioning | Understand Zoom Video SDK versioning scheme, backwards compatibility, new features, breaking changes, and minimum support versions for smooth upgrades.RetryClaude can make mistakes. Please double-check responses.

entry.title: How to build breakout rooms in the Video SDK for web
entry.summary: Tags: Guide, Web | Learn how to implement breakout rooms with Video SDK for Web. Step-by-step tutorial with code examples for session management and participant APIs.

entry.title: How to Extend the MeetingActivity
entry.summary: Tags: Guide, Android | Extend Zoom Android Meeting SDK MeetingActivity for custom functionality. Step-by-step tutorial with code examples and manifest configuration guide.

entry.title: Why use server-to-server OAuth?
entry.summary: Tags: guide | This guide by Elisa Levet explains why the JWT app is being deprecated and encourages users to migrate to the new Server-to-Server OAuth app for more granular scoping options. It provides steps on using the new app type with Postman, facilitating OAuth-authenticated requests between servers without end-user involvement. The guide includes instructions on enabling permissions, creating the app, and obtaining an access token to make API calls. It emphasizes the benefits of scoped access for individual users and suggests referring to the linked resources for further assistance.

entry.title: Introducing Zoom Apps
entry.summary: Tags: announcement | Zoom Apps revolutionize the meeting experience by integrating web applications into Zoom Meetings and Webinars using a JavaScript SDK. You can tailor the app's appearance and functionality, creating a unique in-meeting and post-meeting UX. OAuth mechanisms and the SDK enable granular access control and enhanced meeting experiences. Zoom Apps can appear as side panels, full-screen views, or customized experiences. Features like Meeting Bots, Collaborate Mode, Immersive Mode, and Breakout Rooms API enhance collaboration and interactivity. To get started, create an app on the App Marketplace and explore sample applications. Part 2 covers creating and configuring your app on the Zoom Marketplace.

entry.title: Setting up your Zoom App
entry.summary: Tags: tutorial | This article guides you through the process of creating and configuring a Zoom App. Prerequisites include Ngrok, a Zoom account, and Zoom App Developer permissions. Ngrok is used as a local tunneling tool to expose the local server to the internet. The article covers setting up the app credentials, information, features, and scopes. Once the app is installed, readers are congratulated on creating their Zoom App. The next part of the series will focus on running the app with the provided credentials and Ngrok URL.

entry.title: How long does the App Review Process take?
entry.summary: Tags: App Submission | Zoom App Marketplace review timeline and process explained. 72-hour SLA, submission checklist, developer assessment, and approval requirements.

entry.title: Concurrent video streaming limits on desktop SDKs
entry.summary: Tags: Best practices | Learn Video SDK video subscription limits for custom meeting UIs. Essential reference for concurrent video streams, resolution limits, and view sizes.

entry.title: How to create a user and assign scheduling privilege programmatically
entry.summary: Tags: Best practices | Learn to add meeting assistants with Zoom API. Complete guide to scheduling privilege, assistant management endpoints, and meeting delegation features.

entry.title: How to create a sample JWT for the Meeting SDK
entry.summary: Tags: Guide | Create JWT tokens for Zoom Meeting SDK authentication. Step-by-step tutorial using JWT.io debugger with payload examples and common troubleshooting tips.

entry.title: Fixing a black screen on the Meeting SDK for web
entry.summary: Tags: Troubleshooting, Web | Fix Web Meeting SDK black screen issues with this troubleshooting guide. Step-by-step solutions for display problems and console error resolution.

entry.summary: Tags: Web, Angular, Guide | Integrate Zoom Meeting SDK into Angular apps with this step-by-step guide. Complete tutorial with code examples, installation, and implementation tips.

entry.summary: Tags: Best practices, macOS | Complete macOS Zoom Meeting SDK integration guide for Xcode. Step-by-step setup, verification, troubleshooting, and build configuration instructions.

entry.summary: Tags: Troubleshooting, iOS | Fix iOS screen sharing issues in Zoom SDK apps. Troubleshoot App Group ID, Broadcast Extension setup, and common configuration problems with solutions.
